claimText: Vanuatu approached the International Court of Justice to classify environmental destruction as ecocide.
evidenceExcerpt: Vanuatu approached the International Court of Justice to classify environmental destruction as ecocide.
examUse: Correct-pair question matching countries in news with their recent international environmental actions.
distractorHooks: Replacing Vanuatu with Maldives or Tuvalu
Replacing ICJ with International Criminal Court (ICC) or UN Environment Programme (UNEP)

claimText: The Second WHO Global Conference on Air Pollution and Health was held in Cartagena, Colombia.
evidenceExcerpt: Colombia co-organized the Second WHO Global Conference on Air Pollution and Health in Cartagena.
examUse: Correct-pair question matching global summits to their host cities or countries.
distractorHooks: Replacing Colombia with Switzerland or Kenya
Replacing Cartagena with Geneva or Nairobi

claimText: India and Denmark share a Green Strategic Partnership.
evidenceExcerpt: Denmark: India and Denmark reaffirm Green Strategic Partnership...
examUse: Statement-based question on India's bilateral environmental and strategic partnerships.
distractorHooks: Replacing Denmark with Sweden, Norway, or Germany

claimText: Ethiopia hosted the UN Food Systems Summit +4 Stocktake in Addis Ababa.
evidenceExcerpt: Ethiopia hosted the UN Food Systems Summit +4 Stocktake in Addis Ababa.
examUse: Correct-pair question matching UN summits to their host countries.
distractorHooks: Replacing Ethiopia with Kenya or South Africa
Replacing UN Food Systems Summit with UN Environment Assembly

claimText: Tuvalu signed the 2023 Falepili Union Treaty with Australia to address threats from rising sea levels.
evidenceExcerpt: Tuvalu signed the 2023 Falepili Union Treaty with Australia due to threats from rising sea levels.
examUse: Statement-based question on climate change mitigation treaties and bilateral agreements of Pacific Island nations.
distractorHooks: Replacing Tuvalu with Vanuatu or Fiji
Replacing Australia with New Zealand or the United States

claimText: In Ecuador, indigenous groups have criticized the expansion of oil drilling in the Amazon.
evidenceExcerpt: Ecuador: Indigenous groups criticize oil drilling expansion in the Amazon.
examUse: Statement-based question regarding environmental conflicts and indigenous rights in the Amazon basin.
distractorHooks: Replacing Ecuador with Brazil or Peru
Replacing oil drilling with lithium or gold mining
